claim: For all integers n > 1, n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
predicate: n > 1 and (is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0))

claim: If n > 1 is prime, then (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n.
predicate: n > 1 and (not is_prime(n)) or ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0)

claim: If n > 1 is composite, then (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: n > 1 and (is_prime(n)) or ((factorial(n-1) % n == 0))

claim: For n > 4, (n-1)! is divisible by n if and only if n is composite.
predicate: n > 4 and ((factorial(n-1) % n == 0) == (not is_prime(n)))

claim: If n is prime, then n divides (n-1)! + 1 and does not divide (n-1)!.
predicate: n > 1 and (not is_prime(n)) or (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and (factorial(n-1) % n != 0))

claim: If n is composite and greater than 4, then (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n and n does not divide (n-1)! + 1.
predicate: n > 4 and (is_prime(n)) or ((factorial(n-1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n != 0))

claim: If n is prime, then gcd((n-1)!, n) = 1.
predicate: n > 1 and (not is_prime(n)) or (gcd(factorial(n-1), n) == 1)

claim: If n > 4 is composite, then gcd((n-1)!, n) > 1.
predicate: n > 4 and (is_prime(n)) or (gcd(factorial(n-1), n) > 1)

claim: The number n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n and n is odd.
predicate: n > 1 and (is_prime(n) == ((n % 2 == 1) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0)))

claim: For n > 1, gcd((n-1)!, n) = 1 if and only if n is prime.
predicate: n > 1 and (gcd(factorial(n-1), n) == 1) == is_prime(n)

claim: n is prime if and only if φ(n) = n - 1 and gcd((n-1)!, n) = 1.
predicate: n > 1 and (is_prime(n) == (phi(n) == n - 1 and gcd(factorial(n-1), n) == 1))

claim: If n is prime, then τ(n) = 2 and (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: n > 1 and (not is_prime(n)) or (tau(n) == 2 and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0))

claim: If n is composite and greater than 4, then τ(n) ≥ 3 and (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: n > 4 and (is_prime(n)) or (tau(n) >= 3 and (factorial(n-1) % n == 0))

predicate: n > 4 and (tau(n) == 2) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0)

claim: If n is prime, then σ(n) = n + 1 and (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n.

claim: If n is prime, then vp(n, p) = 1 for p = n and 0 otherwise, and (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n.

claim: If n > 4 is composite, then vp(n, p) ≥ 1 for some p < n and (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: n > 4 and (is_prime(n)) or (any(vp(n, p) >= 1 for p in range(2, n)) and (factorial(n-1) % n == 0))

claim: For n > 4, (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n if and only if n has a prime divisor p < n.
predicate: n > 4 and ((factorial(n-1) % n == 0) == any(vp(n, p) >= 1 for p in range(2, n)))

claim: n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n and n has no prime divisors less than n.
predicate: n > 1 and is_prime(n) == (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and not any(vp(n, p) >= 1 for p in range(2, n)))

claim: For n > 1, gcd((n-1)!, n) = 1 if and only if n has no prime divisors with exponent ≥ 2.
predicate: n > 1 and (gcd(factorial(n-1), n) == 1) == (all(vp(n, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, n)))

claim: For n > 1, n is prime if and only if gcd((n-1)!, n) = 1 and n is square-free.
predicate: n > 1 and is_prime(n) == (gcd(factorial(n-1), n) == 1 and all(vp(n, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, n)))
